title: Orchestrate stop/swap/merge/start/health-check with rollback
action: Create src/update/rollout.rs (declared via `mod rollout;` in src/update/mod.rs) implementing the corrected EP5 flow from approach.md's 'EP5 design correction' section. IMPORTANT: this file lives inside the herdr_go LIBRARY crate (src/update/, under lib.rs's `pub mod update`) -- every cross-module reference inside the lib uses `crate::...`, NEVER `herdr_go::...` (that self-referential form only works from the separate main.rs BINARY crate, e.g. cell -14's main.rs edit correctly uses `herdr_go::config::merge::...` because main.rs is a different crate; rollout.rs must use `crate::doctor::run_service_command` and `crate::config::...` instead). First, two PURE helpers (unit-tested, no network/process): `fn health_check_ok(status: u16) -> bool` (true for any 2xx code); `fn health_check_url(bind_addr: &str) -> String` (extracts the port after the last ':' in bind_addr and returns `format!("http://127.0.0.1:{port}/api/health")`, so a `0.0.0.0`-style bind still targets a connectable loopback address -- note: a bind to a specific non-loopback interface like `192.168.1.5:8787` is a known limitation, out of scope to fix here). Then `pub async fn perform_update(new_bytes: &[u8], config_path: &std::path::Path, bind_addr: &str) -> Result<(), RolloutError>`: (1) `crate::doctor::run_service_command("stop")` -- its exit code is deliberately IGNORED (the service may not have been running yet; that's not an abort condition); (2) `super::swap::backup_and_swap_running_binary(new_bytes)` to get `binary_backup_path`, per D3/D9; (3) self-exec the just-swapped current executable (`std::env::current_exe()`) as a subprocess with arg `--internal-merge-config` and `config_path`, capturing its stdout (the config backup path, if the merge succeeded) -- a nonzero exit or missing backup-path output is logged but is NOT fatal to the update (a failed merge never touches the original config file, per D5/D7's own guarantee -- an unbootable new binary is instead caught by the health check in step 5); (4) `crate::doctor::run_service_command("start")`; (5) poll `health_check_url(bind_addr)` via reqwest GET with a CONCRETE bounded retry budget: 10 attempts, 1 second between attempts (~10 seconds total) -- until `health_check_ok` is true or the budget is exhausted; (6) on success, return Ok(()); (7) on failure (health check never came up within the budget), roll back: restore `binary_backup_path`'s content back onto the current executable path (reuse `swap::backup_and_swap_binary` with the backup file's own content as `new_bytes`), restore the captured config backup content back onto config_path if one was captured in step 3, call `crate::doctor::run_service_command("start")` again and CAPTURE its exit code into the returned error (`RolloutError::RolledBack { restart_exit_code: i32 }`) so a caller can tell whether the rollback-restart itself succeeded -- per D9 as written, health is NOT re-polled after this second start (that end-to-end proof is EP6's smoke test), but the exit code must not be silently discarded. KNOWN LIMITATION to note in a doc comment: on Windows, replacing a running executable's file while the process holding it is still executing is expected to fail (the OS locks the image) -- this is a new, not-yet-proven-safe path distinct from the pre-existing 'Windows service restart unproven' gap; real cross-platform proof is EP6's smoke test, not this cell. This orchestration function is inherently integration-heavy (real subprocesses, real service control, real HTTP) and is NOT unit-tested end-to-end in this cell -- only the two pure helpers above are unit-tested here. Do not wire a CLI verb for perform_update itself (EP6's job) and do not touch src/main.rs in this cell.

truths: health_check_ok returns true for 200/201/204 and false for 404/500
perform_update never references herdr_go:: (it is inside the lib crate and must use crate:: for internal calls)

artifacts.substantive: exports perform_update, health_check_ok, health_check_url, RolloutError (with a RolledBack variant carrying the rollback-restart exit code); perform_update composes crate::doctor::run_service_command, backup_and_swap_running_binary/backup_and_swap_binary, and the internal-merge-config self-exec, with the 2 named pure unit tests present

key_links: perform_update calls crate::doctor::run_service_command for both stop and start -- never reimplements service control
perform_update calls super::swap::backup_and_swap_running_binary (self-update-merge-config-15) for the binary swap -- never reimplements it
perform_update self-execs the current binary with --internal-merge-config (self-update-merge-config-14) rather than calling merge logic directly in-process
prohibitions: No changes to src/main.rs in this cell
No CLI verb wiring for perform_update itself (EP6's job)
Rollback must restore both the binary AND the config backup captured from the internal-merge-config subprocess's stdout, per D9
No herdr_go:: self-references inside this lib-crate file -- use crate:: instead

verify: test -f src/update/rollout.rs && grep -q 'mod rollout' src/update/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn health_check_ok' src/update/rollout.rs && grep -q 'fn health_check_url' src/update/rollout.rs && grep -q 'async fn perform_update' src/update/rollout.rs && grep -q 'crate::doctor::run_service_command' src/update/rollout.rs && grep -q 'internal-merge-config' src/update/rollout.rs && ! grep -q 'herdr_go::' src/update/rollout.rs && cargo test --quiet update

trace.outcome: Added src/update/rollout.rs implementing the corrected EP5 rollout (stop -> swap running binary -> self-exec --internal-merge-config under new binary -> start -> poll /api/health 10x1s -> rollback binary+config+restart on failure). Two pure helpers (health_check_ok, health_check_url) unit-tested; perform_update composes crate::doctor::run_service_command, super::swap::backup_and_swap_running_binary/backup_and_swap_binary, and the internal-merge-config self-exec. RolloutError carries RolledBack{restart_exit_code}. Declared via pub mod rollout; in mod.rs. No herdr_go:: self-refs.
trace.friction: Pre-existing fmt drift in src/update/swap.rs (cell-15's committed file) at HEAD: cargo fmt --all --check flags backup_and_swap_binary signature + current_bytes let-binding. Out of my cell scope (not a reserved file) so left untouched; the wave-close full verify (cargo fmt --all --check) will fail on it until cell-15's file is reformatted.
